title: BABYSITTER_SESSION_ID leak recurred - run:create --harness claude-code bound to wrong session
summary: Issue #136 reports a regression of #133 where `babysitter run:create --harness claude-code` can bind a new run to a stale inherited session ID instead of the active Claude Code session. The run is created successfully but remains stuck at RUN_CREATED because the real Claude Code stop hook checks a different session state file and finds no active run.

observedFailureMode.badBinding: Returned `session.sessionId` and stateFile point to an inherited or freshly generated non-current session instead of the real Claude Code session.
observedFailureMode.runtimeImpact: The stop hook fires for the real Claude session, logs no active loop for that session, exits successfully, and never drives `run:iterate` for the created run.
observedFailureMode.journalEvidence: Affected run journals remain at RUN_CREATED with no STOP_HOOK_INVOKED or EFFECT_REQUESTED events until manually repaired.

triageFindings: `packages/sdk/src/cli/main/runCreate.ts` does delegate to the selected harness adapter via `adapter.resolveSessionId(parsed)` and `adapter.bindSession(...)`.
`packages/sdk/src/utils/sessionMarker.ts` currently checks harness env vars and `AGENT_SESSION_ID` before reading the PID-scoped marker when trust-env is not set.
`packages/sdk/src/harness/README.md` documents the opposite intended precedence: PID-scoped marker first, then harness-native env file/native env var, then `AGENT_SESSION_ID` as lowest priority.

`packages/sdk/src/cli/__tests__/cliRuns.test.ts` contains a regression-shaped test named `binds claude-code runs to AGENT_SESSION_ID when pid markers are disabled` that expects the leaked env session to win even when a marker-backed current session exists.

Claude Code provenance helpers in `packages/sdk/src/harness/adapters/claude-code.ts` currently report only env-var, explicit, or none and should be checked for marker/current-session reporting consistency.
acceptanceFocus: `run:create --harness claude-code` binds to the real current Claude Code session when a live PID-scoped marker/current hook-provided session exists, even if the shell has stale inherited `AGENT_SESSION_ID` or legacy `BABYSITTER_SESSION_ID`-style values.
`run:create`, `session:*`, and `session:whoami` use one coherent resolver contract and report truthful provenance for marker/current-session, env-var, explicit, and none.
The resolver honors the documented trust-env escape hatch: `AGENT_TRUST_ENV_SESSION=1` or `BABYSITTER_TRUST_ENV_SESSION=1` intentionally restores env-first behavior for CI and scripted workflows.

Regression tests cover stale inherited env values losing to current marker/session state, trust-env env-first behavior, JSON/provenance output, and any explicit `--session-id` override semantics changed by the fix.
The stop-hook path for the real Claude Code session can find the run's state file after `run:create`, so orchestration advances beyond RUN_CREATED without manual `run:iterate` or state-file repair.
Diagnostics warn or otherwise expose stale env mismatch cases without breaking JSON automation or making successful normal binding noisy.
No unrelated harnesses, CI workflows, or non-Claude session discovery paths regress.
nonGoals: Do not redesign the orchestration lifecycle outside session ID resolution and binding.
Do not implement a broad stop-hook recovery mechanism for missing session files; that is covered by adjacent issue #166 unless a small shared helper is required.
Do not reintroduce legacy `BABYSITTER_SESSION_ID` as a primary SDK session source unless maintaining compatibility explicitly requires detection-only behavior.
Do not generate shell-oriented process subtasks for this repository workflow.
qualityGates: Regression tests fail against the pre-fix behavior or are clearly shown to encode the previously wrong expectation.
Session marker, whoami, and hook/session-binding tests touched by the fix pass.
SDK test suite passes or any unrelated pre-existing failures are documented with exact evidence.
Compatibility review confirms trust-env CI behavior and non-Claude adapters are preserved.
Final review confirms docs/help text and JSON diagnostics match the implemented resolver semantics.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #136, comments, labels, and related issues/PRs to lock the behavioral spec and scope boundaries.

plannedPhases.name: Session-resolution trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Map run:create, adapter resolution, session:* commands, Claude Code marker/env behavior, bindSession, and stop-hook lookup.

plannedPhases.name: Regression tests
plannedPhases.purpose: Add failing tests that prove current-session marker/session state wins over stale inherited env, while trust-env remains env-first.

plannedPhases.name: Focused implementation
plannedPhases.purpose: Localize the fix in the shared resolver/provenance path and avoid one-off run:create-only behavior.

plannedPhases.name: Verification loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted and full SDK gates, iterating up to three attempts if tests or review find regressions.

plannedPhases.name: Compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Check run:create, session commands, Claude hooks, CI trust-env, JSON automation, and non-Claude adapter behavior.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Confirm acceptance criteria, diagnostics, docs consistency, changed files, and release-note candidate.

breakpoints.when: Only if implementation discovers a real semantic conflict, such as whether explicit `--session-id` should override auto-detected Claude Code sessions despite existing SESSION_ID_CONFLICT behavior.
